species: AEGEAN-169 clade bacterioplankton (SAR11 subclade V / HIMB59 relative)

growth_records.cite: Getz et al. The AEGEAN-169 clade of bacterioplankton is synonymous with SAR11 subclade V (HIMB59) and metabolically distinct. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00179-23. [PMC10308909]
growth_records.snippet: It grew optimally at a seawater salinity of 34 and in salinities as low as 11.6. Its maximum growth rate was 0.02 ± 0.007 divisions per hour at 24°C.
growth_records.notes: Value taken verbatim from text: maximum growth rate 0.02 ± 0.007 'divisions per hour' at 24°C in JW2 medium (isolation/artificial seawater medium, composition not detailed in main text). Recorded rate as-is without converting units; note the paper's unit 'divisions per hour' could denote doublings/h rather than a natural-log-based specific growth rate (mu), so doubling_time_h was NOT back-calculated to avoid introducing an unstated assumption. General paper statement: SAR11/related bacterioplankton are aerobic chemoorganoheterotrophs (oxygen condition inferred from this general statement, not stated specifically for this culture).
